stage.name: What is Cryptocurrency? (Elementary)
stage.description: Beginner-friendly intro to digital money, famous coins, and how to stay safe.

elements.content: Digital money you can use without a bank

Cryptocurrency is digital money — like the coins and bills you use at a store, but it only exists on computers and phones. You can't hold it in your hand, but you can use it to buy things, send it to friends, or save it. The 'crypto' part means it uses secret codes called cryptography to keep everything safe — so only the real owner can spend it.

💻

100% Digital

Lives on computers — no physical coins

🔐

Super Secure

Protected by math no hacker can crack

🌐

Works Everywhere

Send money to anyone in seconds

🤝

No Middleman

Send directly to a friend — no bank needed

actions.text: 🪙 What is Cryptocurrency?
Digital money you can use without a bank

Cryptocurrency is digital money — like the coins and bills you use at a store, but it only exists on computers and phones. You can't hold it in your hand, but you can use it to buy things, send it to friends, or save it. The 'crypto' part means it uses secret codes called cryptography to keep everything safe — so only the real owner can spend it
💻100% DigitalLives on computers — no physical coins
🔐Super SecureProtected by math no hacker can crack

elements.content: There are thousands of cryptocurrencies. Bitcoin was the first (2009) — like digital gold. Ethereum can run programs called smart contracts. XRP is built for super-fast payments — it settles in 3-5 seconds and is what OnlyCrypto uses for payouts!

actions.text: 🏆 Famous Cryptocurrencies
The big names you'll hear everywhere

There are thousands of cryptocurrencies. Bitcoin was the first (2009) — like digital gold. Ethereum can run programs called smart contracts. XRP is built for super-fast payments — it settles in 3-5 seconds and is what OnlyCrypto uses for payouts!
₿Bitcoin (BTC)The original — only 21M will ever exist
ΞEthereum (ETH)Runs apps and smart contracts
✕XRP3-5 sec payments — OnlyCrypto's coin
🌊Thousands moreNew coins launch every day

elements.content: The problem Bitcoin was built to solve

In 2008, a global financial crisis hit when banks failed and millions of people lost their savings. A mysterious person (or group) called Satoshi Nakamoto published a paper describing Bitcoin.


The idea: create a form of money that no bank or government could control, freeze, or inflate away. You hold your own money. No middleman. No permission required.

actions.text: ₿ Why Was Bitcoin Created?
The problem Bitcoin was built to solve

In 2008, a global financial crisis hit when banks failed and millions of people lost their savings. A mysterious person (or group) called Satoshi Nakamoto published a paper describing Bitcoin. The idea: create a form of money that no bank or government could control, freeze, or inflate away. You hold your own money. No middleman. No permission required
📅 2008. Bitcoin whitepaper published
🏦 Problem. Banks failed, people lost money
🔑 Solution. Self-custody, no middleman
🌐 Result. First decentralized currency

elements.content: Why crypto prices go up and down

Like any asset, crypto prices are driven by supply and demand. When more people want to buy than sell, the price goes up. When more people want to sell, the price drops.


Bitcoin has a hard cap of 21 million coins — that is all that will ever exist. Scarcity is a key driver of its value, similar to gold. Ethereum has no hard cap but burns some with every transaction.

actions.text: 📊 Supply, Demand & Value
Why crypto prices go up and down

Like any asset, crypto prices are driven by supply and demand. When more people want to buy than sell, the price goes up. When more people want to sell, the price drops. Bitcoin has a hard cap of 21 million coins — that is all that will ever exist. Scarcity is a key driver of its value, similar to gold. Ethereum has no hard cap but burns some with every transaction
📈 High Demand. Price goes up
📉 High Supply. Price goes down
🔢 21M Max. Bitcoin's hard cap
🔥 Burn. Ethereum reduces supply

elements.content: Not all crypto is the same — here is the difference

Coins have their own blockchain — Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), XRP all run on their own networks.


Tokens are built on top of existing blockchains. For example, thousands of tokens run on Ethereum.


Stablecoins like USDT and USDC are pegged 1:1 to the US dollar. They give you the benefits of crypto (fast, borderless) without the price swings.

actions.text: 🪙 Coins vs Tokens vs Stablecoins
Not all crypto is the same — here is the difference

Coins have their own blockchain — Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), XRP all run on their own networks. Tokens are built on top of existing blockchains. For example, thousands of tokens run on Ethereum. Stablecoins like USDT and USDC are pegged 1:1 to the US dollar. They give you the benefits of crypto (fast, borderless) without the price swings
🔵 Coin. Own blockchain (BTC, ETH, XRP)
🟢 Token. Built on another chain
💵 Stablecoin. Pegged to USD (USDT, USDC)
⚖️ Key Diff. Coins = infrastructure; Tokens = apps

actions.text: Let's explore this interactively. Use the simulation below to experiment with the concepts we just covered.
Try different inputs and see how the outputs change. Hands-on exploration builds intuition faster than reading.

questions.question: What does 'crypto' in cryptocurrency mean?

options.label: It is stored in a physical safe
It uses secret codes (cryptography) to stay secure
It can only be used online for games
It was invented by a government

questions.analysis: 'Crypto' comes from cryptography — the science of using math-based secret codes. These codes make sure only the real owner of a coin can spend it.

questions.question: Which cryptocurrency does OnlyCrypto use for payouts?
questions.analysis: OnlyCrypto uses XRP (the XRP Ledger) because it settles in 3-5 seconds and has nearly zero fees — perfect for automatic payouts.

questions.question: How many Bitcoin will ever exist in total?
questions.analysis: Bitcoin has a hard cap of 21 million coins — ever. This scarcity is one reason people treat it like digital gold.

questions.question: Which of these is TRUE about cryptocurrency?

options.label: A bank must approve every transaction
You need a physical wallet to store it
You can send it to anyone in the world in seconds

questions.analysis: Crypto lets you send money to anyone, anywhere, in seconds — with no bank approval needed. That's what makes it revolutionary.

actions.text: Time to test what you've learned! Answer each question and see how you do.
Take your time — the goal is understanding, not just getting the right answer.
